name: Transactivation Domain I
function: Binds transcriptional machinery and MDM2, the primary regulator of p53 stability.

name: Transactivation Domain II
function: Cooperates with TAD-I; required for full transcriptional output across all target genes.

name: Proline-Rich Region
function: Required specifically for apoptosis induction — distinct from the cell cycle arrest function.

name: DNA Binding Domain
function: Core domain. Physically contacts DNA response elements. Over 80% of all cancer mutations fall here.

name: Linker Region
function: Flexible connector between DNA binding and tetramerization domains.

name: Tetramerization Domain
function: Drives assembly of the active p53 tetramer. p53 must form a 4-unit complex to function.

name: Regulatory Domain
function: Contains post-translational modification sites that fine-tune p53 activity.
